extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won stage 2 via patient positioning, dispatching rivals after Jumbo-Visma's leadout fractured when Groenewegen squeezed Pedersen at 200m.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma's leadout collapsed when Groenewegen drifted right, squeezing teammate Pedersen out at 200m, breaking train continuity.
UAE's Philipsen went absurdly early (300m+), revealing sprint inexperience despite backing up Bennett's wheel throughout final kilometer.
Bennett deliberately conserved throughout stage after feeling weak at 70km, banking energy deficit of rivals chasing position.
Six-rider break squandered 1:17 gap battling golden kilometer sprints at 26.5km, dropping advantage to 57 seconds before regaining minute.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: self-diagnosed poor legs at 70km yet still won comfortably, suggesting superior recovery or positioning efficiency over raw power
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: squeezed out of Jumbo leadout by teammate Groenewegen at critical 200m mark, losing potential stage victory
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: drifted right in sprint finale, compromising teammate Pedersen's positioning despite being in leadout train
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: UAE invested heavily in leadout but Philipsen's 300m+ jump reveals tactical immaturity despite consecutive runner-up finishes
rider_signals.HODEG Alvaro: negative: positioned well on left at 250m but faded to fifth, protesting Groenewegen's blocking afterward
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: negative: wearing race leader jersey but cracked early in sprint, finishing 14th despite early acceleration attempts
